cpt.description: Emergency department visit, high complexity MDM

carc.description: Non-covered services because this is not deemed a 'medical necessity' by the payer.
carc.appealNotes: Frequently overturned when admitting diagnosis clinically supports the service. Cite ACEP/specialty guidelines. ER visits paired with infectious/cardiac/neurologic primary diagnoses are almost always upheld on appeal.

denial.reason: Non-covered services because this is not deemed a 'medical necessity' by the payer.
denial.recommendedAction: Frequently overturned when admitting diagnosis clinically supports the service. Cite ACEP/specialty guidelines. ER visits paired with infectious/cardiac/neurologic primary diagnoses are almost always upheld on appeal.

carc.description: The procedure code is inconsistent with the modifier used or a required modifier is missing.
carc.appealNotes: Often a coding error by the provider. Request itemized justification and ask the provider to resubmit with corrected modifier rather than appealing directly.

patientFacingSummary: Your insurer denied $2127 that may be wrongful — you have 2 appealable denials with a strong chance of reversal. We've drafted a ready-to-send appeal letter to help you recover this amount.
appealLetter:
June 28, 2026

BlueCross BlueShield of Illinois
Appeals Department
PO Box 805107
Chicago, IL 60680

Re: Level 1 Internal Appeal — Claim Number 48291
Member: Jane Q. Smith | Member ID: BCB-2204819334

Dear Appeals Department,

I am writing to formally appeal the denial of Claim Number 48291, processed on June 28, 2026, for services rendered at Memorial Hospital Emergency Department on June 14, 2026.

Specifically, I am appealing the denial of CPT 99285 (Emergency Department Visit, High Complexity Medical Decision Making), billed at $1,847.00, which was denied under reason code CO-50: "not deemed medically necessary." This determination is incorrect.

On June 14, 2026, I presented to the emergency department with symptoms consistent with pneumonia (admitting diagnosis J18.9 — Pneumonia, unspecified organism). An ED visit of high complexity is precisely what the American College of Emergency Physicians (ACEP) guidelines indicate for a patient presenting with acute infectious pulmonary illness requiring evaluation, imaging (CPT 71046), laboratory workup, and intravenous antibiotic therapy (J0696 — ceftriaxone). These services are inseparable from the ED evaluation and collectively confirm that a high-complexity MDM level was both appropriate and medically necessary.

Denying CPT 99285 while the clinical record clearly documents a serious infectious diagnosis, multi-system workup, and IV antibiotic administration is inconsistent with evidence-based standards of care and your plan's own medical necessity criteria.

I respectfully request that BlueCross BlueShield of Illinois overturn the CO-50 denial for CPT 99285 and reprocess the claim for payment.

Please respond within 30 days as required by applicable regulations. For questions, I can be reached at jwalinshah13@gmail.com.

Sincerely,
Jane Q. Smith
Member ID: BCB-2204819334
